subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Marine Toxins and Jellyfish
description: A quick win: understand Marine Toxins and Jellyfish.

hook.text: The box jellyfish delivers enough venom to kill 60 adults. Death can come in 2 minutes.

buildup.text: Jellyfish tentacles are lined with cnidocytes—cells that fire venomous harpoons on contact.

discovery.text: Box jellyfish venom attacks the heart, nervous system, and skin cells simultaneously.

twist.text: Vinegar deactivates unfired cnidocytes on the skin—fresh water makes them fire more.

climax.text: In Australia, beaches in jellyfish season stock vinegar and compression bandages.

punchline.text: Don't rinse with fresh water. Vinegar first. The ocean teaches hard lessons.

quiz.question: What should you apply to jellyfish stings to deactivate unfired stinging cells?
